steps.title: Identify the present-time signal
steps.content: ”Currently” directly indicates that the painting's location and state at this moment in time are being described.

steps.title: Apply the present-tense rule
steps.content:
Simple present tense expresses what is currently true. “hangs” is simple present and correctly describes the painting's location at the museum. “will have hung,” “hung,” and “was hanging” all use past or future tenses that contradict the “Currently” signal.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
